description: A structured semantic taxonomy for data-centric faults in IoT-Edge-Cloud (IEC) deployments. IoDFT characterises faults through six independent facets and a three-stage annotation procedure.
reference: Altarrazi et al., Addressing the Faults Landscape in the Internet of Things: Toward Datacentric and System Resilience, IEEE Internet Computing, 27(6), 2023.

stage_1.name: Base Facets
stage_1.description: Assign the four base facets that characterise the fault origin and structure.

stage_2.name: Data Pitfall
stage_2.description: Identify the observable data manifestation of the fault.

stage_3.name: Possible Cause
stage_3.description: Hypothesise the underlying cause linked to the component, to guide monitoring and diagnosis.

component.description: Where the fault originates within the IEC system.

source.description: The location and scope of the fault origin.

duration.description: How long the fault persists in the system.

type.description: The structural characteristic of the anomaly in the data.

pitfall.description: The observable data manifestation of the fault.

cause.description: Hypothesised underlying reason, linked to the component facet.
